raw_consumer_statement:
Dear CFPB team, In late XX/XX/XXXX, I mistakenly selected a {$20000.00} balance transfer option while opening a new Hawaiian Airlines credit card with Barclays. I attempted to cancel the transfer, but was informed that it had already been processed by XXXX and forwarded to Barclays. As such, I completed a full repayment of {$20000.00} to Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX, effectively closing the matter on my end. 

However, I recently reviewed my XX/XX/XXXX XXXX credit card statement and discovered a reversal of the {$20000.00} balance transfer. Upon contacting XXXX customer service, I was informed that this reversal was requested by Barclays. 

Given that I already paid the full {$20000.00} amount to Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX, this reversal appears to be a duplicate charge resulting from a potential system error between XXXX and Barclays. I am requesting immediate investigation and correction of this matter. 

For your reference, I previously submitted a complaint to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) regarding this issue on XX/XX/XXXX. The complaint number is XXXX. 

Please review this matter urgently and confirm that appropriate corrective actions will be taken to prevent any financial harm or credit impact. 

Sincerely, XXXX
